title: What Drake Maye, Milton Williams said of A.J. Brown’s potential arrival in New England
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots and Eagles are reportedly close to finalizing a trade involving A.J. Brown after June 1.
A trade post-June 1 would reduce the Eagles’ cap hit from $43.3 million to $16.3 million.
Patriots' players, including Drake Maye and Milton Williams, view A.J. Brown as a valuable potential addition.

title: Something fishy? Large groups of dead fish spotted in Boston, Worcester ponds
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dead fish have been observed at Leverett Pond in Boston and Worcester, attributed to natural seasonal changes.
Massachusetts Environmental Police and MassWildlife report fish kills due to elevated temperatures and reduced oxygen levels.
Large fish kill of around 1,000 fish was documented in Worcester's University Park linked to low water and high temperatures.

title: N.H. man killed his mother in murder-suicide, officials say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Thomas Wallace, 57, killed his mother Eileen Shevelin, 78, in a murder-suicide in Dover, New Hampshire.
Shevelin died by asphyxiation and Wallace died from a gunshot wound, officials confirmed following an autopsy.
Police discovered the bodies during a welfare check on May 26; family requests privacy, no further details expected.

title: East Boston couple facing civil rights charges after alleged racist attack on group dining at restaurant
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Arielle and Gregory Raso face civil rights and assault charges after an alleged racist attack on Hispanic diners in East Boston.
The Rasos allegedly used racial slurs, threw a fence piece, and sprayed water at diners over a noise complaint.
Arraigned in Boston Municipal Court, the couple posted $750 bail each and must stay away from the victims and restaurant.

title: What Craig Breslow said about the Red Sox potentially selling off pieces amid continued struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox are committed to improving performance and not selling off key players despite current struggles in the 2026 season.
Craig Breslow expressed confidence in the team, focusing on turning around their performance rather than trading assets.
Boston ranks last in MLB in runs scored, struggling with consistent offensive production despite adequate pitching and defense.

title: Fall River police looking for man accused of destroying shooting victim’s memorial
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Fall River police seek William Acosta Abreu, 20, for vandalizing a memorial for 2023 murder victim Diamonte Odom.
Acosta Abreu, identified by his tattoos, filmed destroying the memorial; multiple arrest warrants are issued against him.
Anyone with information on Acosta Abreu's whereabouts should contact Fall River police at (508) 676-8511; anonymity assured.

title: 5 takeaways from Patriots’ first 2026 OTA practice
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye completes 13-of-22 passes with one interception during Patriots' first 2026 OTA practice.
Key players Christian Gonzalez and Kayshon Boutte absent from Patriots' first OTA session.
Cory Durden steps into starting nose tackle role during practice, following Khyiris Tonga's departure.

title: Woody’s Grill & Tap in Fenway will permanently close
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Woody's Grill & Tap in Fenway will close permanently on Thursday, May 28, after serving its final customers.
Located at 58 Hemenway St., Woody's was known for wood-fired pizza and hosted community events for years.
A Reddit announcement about the closure garnered nostalgic responses, praising its pizzas and neighborhood atmosphere.

title: A royal family dined at a popular Somerville restaurant this week
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Belgium's royal family dined at Sarma Restaurant in Somerville to celebrate Princess Elisabeth's Harvard graduation.
Sarma, a popular Greater Boston restaurant, confirmed the royal visit but did not specify menu details or family members present.
Princess Elisabeth, heir to the Belgian throne, graduated with a master's in public policy from Harvard Kennedy School.

title: What to know about the rejection of Healey’s Parole Board nominee as backlash builds
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts Governor's Council narrowly rejected Vincent DeMore, a prosecutor, as Gov. Maura Healey's Parole Board nominee.
The rejection followed advocacy by Healey and district attorneys, emphasizing DeMore's prosecutorial and victim-centered experience.
Concerns about parole system scrutiny rose following a Cambridge shooting involving a parolee, highlighting board's decision implications.
